This module provides comprehensive syntax error detection for YAML files.\n4\t//! It detects three main categories of errors:\n5\t//!\n6\t//! 1. **Indentation Errors**: Mixed spaces/tabs, inconsistent indentation levels\n7\t//! 2. **Delimiter Errors**: Missing colons, unbalanced brackets/braces, quote errors\n8\t//! 3. **Structure Errors**: Invalid mappings, malformed sequences, duplicate keys\n9\t//!\n10\t//! ## Architecture\n11\t//!\n12\t//! The detector follows a line-by-line analysis approach:\n13\t//!\n14\t//! - LineTracker: Tracks line numbers and content for error reporting\n15\t//! - IndentationDetector: Analyzes indentation patterns\n16\t//! - DelimiterDetector: Checks delimiter consistency\n17\t//! - StructureDetector: Validates YAML structure rules\n18\t//!\n19\t//! ## Usage\n20\t//!\n21\t//! ```ignore\n22\t//! use Eq)]\n42\tpub enum IndentationErrorType {\n43\t    /// Mixed tabs and spaces in the same line\n44\t    MixedTabsAndSpaces,\n45\t    /// Indentation not a multiple of the base indent size\n46\t    InvalidIndentLevel,\n47\t    /// Indentation increase is too large (> 2 levels at once)\n48\t    ExcessiveIndentIncrease,\n49\t    /// Indentation increase is not a multiple of base indent size\n50\t    InvalidIndentIncrease,\n51\t    /// Tab character detected (when tabs are not allowed)\n52\t    TabCharacter,\n53\t}\n54\t\n55\timpl IndentationErrorType {\n56\t    /// Get a human-readable indentation\",\n60\t            Self::InvalidIndentLevel => \"indentation level is not a multiple of base indent size\",\n61\t            Self::ExcessiveIndentIncrease => \"indentation increase is too large\",\n62\t            Self::InvalidIndentIncrease => \"indentation increase is not a multiple of base indent size\",\n63\t            Self::TabCharacter => \"tab character detected in indentation\",\n64\t        }\n65\t    }\n66\t\n67\t    /// Get a short code for this error type\n68\t    pub fn code(&self) -> &'static str {\n69\t        match self {\n70\t            Self::MixedTabsAndSpaces => \"E001\",\n71\t            Self::InvalidIndentLevel => \"E002\",\n72\t            Self::ExcessiveIndentIncrease => \"E003\",\n73\t            Self::InvalidIndentIncrease => \"E004\",\n74\t            Self::TabCharacter => \"E005\",\n75\t        }\n76\t    }\n77\t\n78\t    /// Get all indentation error types\n79\t    pub fn all() -> &'static [Self] {\n80\t        &[\n81\t            Self::MixedTabsAndSpaces,\n82\t            Self::InvalidIndentLevel,\n83\t            Self::ExcessiveIndentIncrease,\n84\t            Self::InvalidIndentIncrease,\n85\t            Self::TabCharacter,\n86\t        ]\n87\t    }\n88\t}\n89\t\n90\timpl fmt::Display for IndentationErrorType {\n91\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n92\t        write!(f, \"{}: {}\", self.code(), self.description())\n93\t    }\n94\t}\n95\t\n96\t/// Classification of delimiter error types\n97\t///\n98\t/// This enum provides structured categorization of different delimiter\n99\t/// error types that can occur in YAML files.\n100\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n101\tpub enum DelimiterErrorType {\n102\t    /// Missing colon after a key\n103\t    MissingColon,\n104\t    /// Unmatched opening bracket '['\n105\t    UnmatchedOpeningBracket,\n106\t    /// Unmatched closing bracket ']'\n107\t    UnmatchedClosingBracket,\n108\t    /// Unclosed bracket '['\n109\t    UnclosedBracket,\n110\t    /// Unmatched opening brace '{'\n111\t    UnmatchedOpeningBrace,\n112\t    /// Unmatched closing brace '}'\n113\t    UnmatchedClosingBrace,\n114\t    /// Unclosed brace '{'\n115\t    UnclosedBrace,\n116\t    /// Mismatched quotes (single inside double or vice versa)\n117\t    MismatchedQuotes,\n118\t    /// Unclosed single quote\n119\t    UnclosedSingleQuote,\n120\t    /// Unclosed double quote\n121\t    UnclosedDoubleQuote,\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\timpl DelimiterErrorType {\n125\t    /// Get a human-readable description of this error Self::UnmatchedOpeningBracket => \"unmatched opening bracket '['\",\n130\t            Self::UnmatchedClosingBracket => \"unmatched closing bracket ']'\",\n131\t            Self::UnclosedBracket => Self::UnmatchedClosingBrace,\n166\t            Self::UnclosedBrace,\n167\t            Self::MismatchedQuotes,\n168\t            Self::UnclosedSingleQuote,\n169\t            Self::UnclosedDoubleQuote,\n170\t        ]\n171\t    }\n172\t}\n173\t\n174\timpl fmt::Display for DelimiterErrorType {\n175\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n176\t        write!(f, \"{}: {}\", self.code(), self.description())\n177\t    }\n178\t}\n179\t\n180\t/// Main syntax detector for YAML content\n181\t///\n182\t/// This struct orchestrates all syntax detection checks and provides\n183\t/// a unified interface for error detection.\n184\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n185\tpub struct SyntaxDetector {\n186\t    /// Configuration for detection behavior\n187\t    config: DetectorConfig,\n188\t    /// Track indentation state across lines\n189\t    indentation_state: IndentationState,\n190\t    /// Track delimiter state (brackets, braces, quotes)\n191\t    delimiter_state: DelimiterState,\n192\t    /// Track structure state (keys, sequences, mappings)\n193\t    structure_state: StructureState,\n194\t}\n195\t\n196\t/// Configuration for syntax detection behavior\n197\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n198\tpub struct DetectorConfig {\n199\t    /// Whether to detect tab/space mixing\n200\t    pub detect_mixed_indentation: bool,\n201\t    /// Whether to check for consistent indentation (multiples of base)\n202\t    pub check_consistent_indentation: bool,\n203\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4)\n204\t    pub base_indent_size: usize,\n205\t    /// Whether to validate delimiter balancing\n206\t    pub validate_delimiter_balance: bool,\n207\t    /// Whether to detect duplicate keys\n208\t    pub detect_duplicate_keys: bool,\n209\t    /// Whether to detect invalid sequence syntax\n210\t    pub detect_invalid_sequences: bool,\n211\t    /// Whether to detect invalid mapping syntax\n212\t    pub detect_invalid_mappings: bool,\n213\t}\n214\t\n215\timpl Default for DetectorConfig {\n216\t    fn default() -> Self {\n217\t        Self {\n218\t            detect_mixed_indentation: true,\n219\t            check_consistent_indentation: true,\n220\t            base_indent_size: 2,\n221\t            validate_delimiter_balance: true,\n222\t            detect_duplicate_keys: true,\n223\t            detect_invalid_sequences: true,\n224\t            detect_invalid_mappings: true,\n225\t        }\n226\t    }\n227\t}\n228\t\n229\t/// State tracking for indentation analysis\n230\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n231\tstruct IndentationState {\n232\t    /// Whether tabs have been seen in the file\n233\t    has_tabs: bool,\n234\t    /// Whether spaces have been seen in the file\n235\t    has_spaces: bool,\n236\t    /// Previous line's indentation level (in spaces)\n237\t    prev_indent_level: usize,\n238\t    /// Expected indentation levels for nested contexts\n239\t    indent_stack: Vec<usize>,\n240\t    /// Lines with mixed indentation\n241\t    mixed_lines: Vec<usize>,\n242\t}\n243\t\n244\t/// State tracking for delimiter analysis\n245\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n246\tstruct DelimiterState {\n247\t    /// Stack of opening brackets/braces\n248\t    bracket_stack: Vec<(char, usize)>, // (bracket_type, line_number)\n249\t    /// Current quote state (None, Single, Double)\n250\t    quote_state: Option<QuoteType>,\n251\t    /// Lines with quote errors\n252\t    quote_errors: Vec<usize>,\n253\t    /// Whether we're inside a multiline block (|, >)\n254\t    in_multiline_block: bool,\n255\t    /// Indentation level of the multiline block start\n256\t    multiline_block_indent: usize,\n257\t    /// Whether we're inside flow-style context (within [] or {})\n258\t    in_flow_context: bool,\n259\t}\n260\t\n261\t/// Quote type for tracking\n262\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n263\tenum QuoteType {\n264\t    Single,\n265\t    Double,\n266\t}\n267\t\n268\t/// State tracking for structure analysis\n269\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n270\tstruct StructureState {\n271\t    /// Stack of nested structures (mapping, sequence, etc.)\n272\t    context_stack: Vec<StructureContext>,\n273\t    /// Stack of key scopes, one per mapping scope\n274\t    /// Each scope contains: (keys_set, indent_level)\n275\t    /// The top of the stack is the current scope\n276\t    key_scope_stack: Vec<(HashSet<String>, usize)>,\n277\t    /// Previous line's indentation level\n278\t    prev_indent: usize,\n279\t    /// Previous line's parent key status\n280\t    prev_was_parent_key: bool,\n281\t}\n282\t\n283\t/// Current structure context\n284\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n285\tenum StructureContext {\n286\t    Mapping,\n287\t    Sequence,\n288\t    FlowMapping,\n289\t    FlowSequence,\n290\t}\n291\t\n292\timpl SyntaxDetector {\n293\t    /// Create a new syntax detector with default configuration\n294\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n295\t        Self::with_config(DetectorConfig::default())\n296\t    }\n297\t\n298\t    /// Create a new syntax detector with custom configuration\n299\t    pub fn with_config(config: DetectorConfig) -> Self {\n300\t        Self {\n301\t            config,\n302\t            indentation_state: IndentationState::default(),\n303\t            delimiter_state: DelimiterState::default(),\n304\t            structure_state: StructureState::default(),\n305\t        }\n306\t    }\n307\t\n308\t    /// Detect all syntax errors in YAML content\n309\t    ///\n310\t    /// This is the main entry point for syntax detection. It runs all\n311\t    /// enabled detectors and returns a comprehensive list of errors.\n312\t    ///\n313\t    /// # Arguments\n314\t    /// * `content` - The YAML content to analyze\n315\t    ///\n316\t    /// # Returns\n317\t    /// A vector of validation errors representing all detected syntax issues\n318\t    pub fn detect_errors(&mut self, content: &str) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n319\t        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n320\t        let lines: Vec<&str> = content.lines().collect();\n321\t\n322\t        // Reset state for new detection\n323\t        self.reset_state();\n324\t\n325\t        for (line_num, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {\n326\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1; // Convert to 1-indexed\n327\t\n328\t            // Skip empty lines and comments for most checks\n329\t            let is_meaningful = !line.trim().is_empty() && !line.trim().starts_with('#');\n330\t\n331\t            if is_meaningful {\n332\t                // Run all enabled detectors\n333\t                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation || self.config.check_consistent_indentation {\n334\t                    self.detect_indentation_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n335\t                }\n336\t\n337\t                if self.config.validate_delimiter_balance {\n338\t                    self.detect_delimiter_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n339\t                }\n340\t\n341\t                if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences || self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n342\t                    self.detect_structure_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n343\t                }\n344\t\n345\t                if self.config.detect_duplicate_keys {\n346\t                    self.detect_duplicate_key_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n347\t                }\n348\t            }\n349\t        }\n350\t\n351\t        // Final validation checks\n352\t        self.finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut errors);\n353\t        self.finalize_structure_checks(&mut errors);\n354\t\n355\t        errors\n356\t    }\n357\t\n358\t    /// Detect syntax errors and convert to ValidationResult\n359\t    pub fn detect_to_validation_result(&mut self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n360\t        let errors = self.detect_errors(content);\n361\t\n362\t        if errors.is_empty() {\n363\t            ValidationResult::success()\n364\t        } else {\n365\t            ValidationResult::failure(errors)\n366\t        }\n367\t    }\n368\t\n369\t    /// Reset all state tracking for a new detection run\n370\t    fn reset_state(&mut self) {\n371\t        self.indentation_state = IndentationState::default();\n372\t        self.delimiter_state = DelimiterState::default();\n373\t        self.structure_state = StructureState::default();\n374\t    }\n375\t\n376\t    /// Detect indentation-related errors\n377\t    fn detect_indentation_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n378\t        let leading_whitespace_len = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n379\t\n380\t        // Check for mixed tabs and spaces in leading whitespace\n381\t        if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n382\t            let leading_chars: Vec<char> = line.chars().take(leading_whitespace_len).collect();\n383\t            let has_tabs = leading_chars.contains(&'\\t');\n384\t            let has_spaces = leading_chars.contains(&' ');\n385\t\n386\t            if has_tabs && has_spaces {\n387\t                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n388\t                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n389\t                self.indentation_state.mixed_lines.push(line_num);\n390\t\n391\t                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::MixedTabsAndSpaces;\n392\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n393\t                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n394\t                    error_type.description()\n395\t                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n396\t            } else if has_tabs {\n397\t                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n398\t                // Optionally report tab-only indentation\n399\t                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation {\n400\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::TabCharacter;\n401\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n402\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n403\t                        format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n404\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n405\t                }\n406\t            } else if has_spaces {\n407\t                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n408\t            }\n409\t        }\n410\t\n411\t        // Check for consistent indentation levels\n412\t        if self.config.check_consistent_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n413\t            let indent_level = leading_whitespace_len;\n414\t\n415\t            // Check if indentation is a multiple of base_indent_size\n416\t            if indent_level % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n417\t                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentLevel;\n418\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n419\t                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n420\t                    format!(\"{}: {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n421\t                            error_type.code(), indent_level, self.config.base_indent_size)\n422\t                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n423\t            }\n424\t\n425\t            // Check if indentation increase is consistent\n426\t            if indent_level > self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n427\t                let increase = indent_level - self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level;\n428\t\n429\t                // Check if increase is too large (more than 2 levels at once)\n430\t                if increase > self.config.base_indent_size * 2 {\n431\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::ExcessiveIndentIncrease;\n432\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n433\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n434\t                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces exceeds maximum of {} levels\",\n435\t                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size * 2)\n436\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n437\t                }\n438\t\n439\t                if increase % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n440\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentIncrease;\n441\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n442\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n443\t                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n444\t                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size)\n445\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n446\t                } else {\n447\t                    // Valid increase - push to stack\n448\t                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.push(indent_level);\n449\t                }\n450\t            } else if indent_level < self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n451\t                // Decrease in indentation - pop from stack\n452\t                while !self.indentation_state.indent_stack.is_empty()\n453\t                      && *self.indentation_state.indent_stack.last().unwrap() > indent_level {\n454\t                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.pop();\n455\t                }\n456\t            }\n457\t\n458\t            self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level = indent_level;\n459\t        }\n460\t    }\n461\t\n462\t    /// Detect delimiter-related errors\n463\t    fn detect_delimiter_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n464\t        let leading_whitespace_len = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n465\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n466\t\n467\t        // Check if we're inside a multiline block\n468\t        if self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block {\n469\t            // Multiline blocks continue until we find a line with less or equal indentation\n470\t            // that's not empty or a comment\n471\t            if leading_whitespace_len > self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent ||\n472\t               (leading_whitespace_len == self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent && (trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#'))) {\n473\t                return; // Skip lines inside the multiline block\n474\t            } else {\n475\t                // Exit multiline block when indentation decreases\n476\t                self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block = false;\n477\t                self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent = 0;\n478\t            }\n479\t        }\n480\t\n481\t        // Skip empty lines and comments (but not when inside multiline blocks - handled above)\n482\t        if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n483\t            return;\n484\t        }\n485\t\n486\t        // Handle document markers (---, ...)\n487\t        if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n488\t            return; // Document markers should not be flagged for missing colons\n489\t        }\n490\t\n491\t        // Handle multiline block scalars (|, >, |-, |- , >-, >+, |+)\n492\t        // These appear after a colon, like \"key: |\" or \"key: >\"\n493\t        if trimmed.contains(\": |\") || trimmed.contains(\": >\") ||\n494\t           trimmed.contains(\":|-\") || trimmed.contains(\":>-\") ||\n495\t           trimmed.contains(\":|+\") || trimmed.contains(\":>+\") {\n496\t            self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block = true;\n497\t            self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent = leading_whitespace_len; // Content must be MORE indented than this line\n498\t        }\n499\t\n500\t        // Check for missing colons after keys\n501\t        if !trimmed.starts_with('-') && // Not a sequence item\n502\t           !trimmed.starts_with('?') && // Not an explicit key\n503\t           !trimmed.starts_with(':') && // Not a value\n504\t           !trimmed.starts_with('&') && // Not an anchor\n505\t           !trimmed.starts_with('*') && // Not an alias\n506\t           !trimmed.starts_with('!') && // Not a tag\n507\t           !trimmed.starts_with('|') && // Not a literal block scalar\n508\t           !trimmed.starts_with('>') && // Not a folded block scalar\n509\t           !trimmed.contains('{') && // Not flow style mapping\n510\t           !trimmed.contains('[') && // Not flow style sequence\n511\t           !trimmed.contains(':') && // No colon at all\n512\t           !trimmed.is_empty() &&\n513\t           self.looks_like_key(trimmed) {\n514\t\n515\t            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MissingColon;\n516\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n517\t                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n518\t                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n519\t            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n520\t        }\n521\t\n522\t        // Track bracket/brace balance and flow context\n523\t        for (char_pos, ch) in line.chars().enumerate() {\n524\t            match ch {\n525\t                '[' => {\n526\t                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('[', line_num));\n527\t                    self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = true;\n528\t                }\n529\t                ']' => {\n530\t                    if let Some(('[', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n531\t                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n532\t                        // Update flow context when stack becomes empty\n533\t                        self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = !self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.is_empty();\n534\t                    } else {\n535\t                        let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::UnmatchedClosingBracket;\n536\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n537\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n538\t                            format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n539\t                        ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n540\t                    }\n541\t                }\n542\t                '{' => {\n543\t                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('{', line_num));\n544\t                    self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = true;\n545\t                }\n546\t                '}' => {\n547\t                    if let Some(('{', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n548\t                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n549\t                        // Update flow context when stack becomes empty\n550\t                        self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = !self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.is_empty();\n551\t                    } else {\n552\t                        let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::UnmatchedClosingBrace;\n553\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n554\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n555\t                            format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n556\t                        ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n557\t                    }\n558\t                }\n559\t                '\\'' => {\n560\t                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n561\t                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Single),\n562\t                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n563\t                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => {\n564\t                            // Mismatched quote\n565\t                            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MismatchedQuotes;\n566\t                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n567\t                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n568\t                                format!(\"{}: {} (single inside double)\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n569\t                            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n570\t                            self.delimiter_state.quote_errors.push(line_num);\n571\t                        }\n572\t                    }\n573\t                }\n574\t                '\"' => {\n575\t                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n576\t                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Double),\n577\t                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n578\t                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => {\n579\t                            // Mismatched quote\n580\t                            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MismatchedQuotes;\n581\t                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n582\t                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n583\t                                format!(\"{}: {} (double inside single)\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n584\t                            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n585\t                            self.delimiter_state.quote_errors.push(line_num);\n586\t                        }\n587\t                    }\n588\t                }\n589\t                _ => {}\n590\t            }\n591\t        }\n592\t    }\n593\t\n594\t    /// Detect structure-related errors\n595\t    fn detect_structure_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n596\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n597\t\n598\t        // Detect invalid sequence syntax\n599\t        if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences {\n600\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n601\t                // Valid sequence item\n602\t                if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n603\t                   !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Sequence)) {\n604\t                    self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Sequence);\n605\t                }\n606\t            } else if trimmed.starts_with('-') && trimmed.len() > 1 {\n607\t                // Dash followed by something other than space\n608\t                if let Some(next_char) = trimmed.chars().nth(1) {\n609\t                    if !next_char.is_whitespace() {\n610\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n611\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n612\t                            \"sequence dash '-' must be followed by whitespace\"\n613\t                        ).with_line(line_num));\n614\t                    }\n615\t                }\n616\t            }\n617\t        }\n618\t\n619\t        // Detect invalid mapping syntax\n620\t        if self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n621\t            if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n622\t                let before_colon = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n623\t\n624\t                // Check if colon is properly used after a key\n625\t                if !before_colon.is_empty() && !before_colon.ends_with(' ') {\n626\t                    // Valid key: value pair\n627\t                    if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n628\t                       !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Mapping)) {\n629\t                        self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Mapping);\n630\t                    }\n631\t                } else if before_colon.is_empty() {\n632\t                    // Colon at start - might be a value or error\n633\t                    if !trimmed.starts_with(\": \") && trimmed != \":\" {\n634\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n635\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n636\t                            \"colon ':' at start must be followed by space\"\n637\t                        ).with_line(line_num));\n638\t                    }\n639\t                }\n640\t            }\n641\t        }\n642\t\n643\t        // Check for flow syntax indicators\n644\t        if trimmed.contains('{') || trimmed.contains('[') {\n645\t            self.structure_state.context_stack.push(if trimmed.contains('{') {\n646\t                StructureContext::FlowMapping\n647\t            } else {\n648\t                StructureContext::FlowSequence\n649\t            });\n650\t        }\n651\t    }\n652\t\n653\t    /// Detect duplicate key errors\n654\t    fn detect_duplicate_key_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n655\t        // Skip duplicate key detection when inside flow-style contexts ([] or {})\n656\t        // Flow-style YAML uses {key: value} syntax which should not be treated as duplicate keys\n657\t        if self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context {\n658\t            return;\n659\t        }\n660\t\n661\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n662\t        let indent = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n663\t\n664\t        // Handle sequence items (lines starting with \"- \")\n665\t        // Each sequence item creates its own scope for its children\n666\t        if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n667\t            // Sequence item: pop all scopes at this indent level or deeper\n668\t            // This handles sibling sequence items (e.g., multiple \"-\" at same indent)\n669\t            while !self.structure_state.key_scope_stack.is_empty() {\n670\t                if let Some((_, scope_indent)) = self.structure_state.key_scope_stack.last() {\n671\t                    if *scope_indent >= indent {\n672\t                        // Pop scopes at same or deeper level (siblings or nested)\n673\t                        self.structure_state.key_scope_stack.pop();\n674\t                    } else {\n675\t                        // Keep parent scopes\n676\t                        break;\n677\t                    }\n678\t                } else {\n679\t                    break;\n680\t                }\n681\t            }\n682\t            // Create a new scope for this sequence item's children\n683\t            self.structure_state.key_scope_stack.push((HashSet::new(), indent));\n684\t\n685\t            // Update previous indentation for next comparison\n686\t            self.structure_state.prev_indent = indent;\n687\t            return;\n688\t        }\n689\t\n690\t        // Extract key if this is a key-value pair or parent key\n691\t        if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n692\t            let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n693\t\n694\t            // Check if this is a parent key (ends with colon and no value on same line)\n695\t            // A parent key typically has nothing after the colon or just whitespace/comments\n696\t            let after_colon = trimmed[colon_pos + 1..].trim();\n697\t            let is_parent_key = after_colon.is_empty() ||\n698\t                                after_colon.starts_with('#');\n699\t\n700\t            // Skip if it's a value or not a simple key\n701\t            if key_part.is_empty() || key_part.contains('-') || key_part.contains('?') {\n702\t                // Still need to update prev_indent even if we skip this line\n703\t                self.structure_state.prev_indent = indent;\n704\t                return;\n705\t            }\n706\t\n707\t            let key = key_part.trim();\n708\t\n709\t            // Skip quoted keys and special keys\n710\t            if key.starts_with('\\'') || key.starts_with('\"') || key.contains('#') {\n711\t                self.structure_state.prev_indent = indent;\n712\t                return;\n713\t            }\n714\t\n715\t            if is_parent_key {\n716\t                // Parent key: pop all scopes at this indent level or deeper, then prepare for new scope\n717\t                while !self.structure_state.key_scope_stack.is_empty() {\n718\t                    if let Some((_, scope_indent)) = self.structure_state.key_scope_stack.last() {\n719\t                        if *scope_indent >= indent {\n720\t                            // Pop scopes at same or deeper level (siblings or nested)\n721\t                            self.structure_state.key_scope_stack.pop();\n722\t                        } else {\n723\t                            // Keep parent scopes\n724\t                            break;\n725\t                        }\n726\t                    } else {\n727\t                        break;\n728\t                    }\n729\t                }\n730\t                // Create a new scope for this parent key's children\n731\t                self.structure_state.key_scope_stack.push((HashSet::new(), indent));\n732\t            } else {\n733\t                // Key-value pair: add to current scope\n734\t                // Ensure root scope exists\n735\t                if self.structure_state.key_scope_stack.is_empty() {\n736\t                    self.structure_state.key_scope_stack.push((HashSet::new(), 0));\n737\t                }\n738\t\n739\t                if let Some((current_scope, scope_indent)) = self.structure_state.key_scope_stack.last_mut() {\n740\t                    // Key-value pairs belong to the current scope (the one created by their parent key)\n741\t                    // regardless of their indentation level, as long as they're at or deeper than the scope's indent\n742\t                    if indent >= *scope_indent {\n743\t                        if current_scope.contains(key) {\n744\t                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n745\t                                format!(\"key_{}\", key),\n746\t                                format!(\"duplicate key '{}' in the same mapping scope\", key)\n747\t                            ).with_line(line_num));\n748\t                        } else {\n749\t                            current_scope.insert(key.to_string());\n750\t                        }\n751\t                    }\n752\t                }\n753\t            }\n754\t        }\n755\t\n756\t        // Update previous indentation for next comparison\n757\t        self.structure_state.prev_indent = indent;\n758\t    }\n759\t\n760\t    /// Finalize delimiter checks after processing all lines\n761\t    fn finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut self, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n762\t        // Check for unclosed brackets\n763\t        for (bracket, line_num) in &self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack {\n764\t            let error_type = match *bracket {\n765\t                '[' => DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedBracket,\n766\t                '{' => DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedBrace,\n767\t                _ => continue, // Skip unknown bracket types\n768\t            };\n769\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n770\t                \"delimiter_balance\".to_string(),\n771\t                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n772\t            ).with_line(*line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n773\t        }\n774\t\n775\t        // Check for unclosed quotes\n776\t        if self.delimiter_state.quote_state.is_some() {\n777\t            let (error_type, quote_char) = match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n778\t                Some(QuoteType::Single) => (DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedSingleQuote, \"'\"),\n779\t                Some(QuoteType::Double) => (DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedDoubleQuote, \"\\\"\"),\n780\t                None => unreachable!(),\n781\t            };\n782\t\n783\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n784\t                \"quote_balance\".to_string(),\n785\t                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n786\t            ).with_line(0).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type)); // Line 0 since we don't track where it opened\n787\t        }\n788\t    }\n789\t\n790\t    /// Finalize structure checks after processing all lines\n791\t    fn finalize_structure_checks(&mut self, _errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n792\t        // No-op - same-level duplicate detection is handled in detect_duplicate_key_errors\n793\t    }\n794\t\n795\t    /// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n796\t    fn get_leading_whitespace_length(&self, line: &str) -> usize {\n797\t        line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n798\t    }\n799\t\n800\t    /// Check if a line looks like a key definition\n801\t    fn looks_like_key(&self, trimmed: &str) -> bool {\n802\t        // Keys are typically alphanumeric with underscores, dashes, or dots\n803\t        // They don't start with special YAML characters\n804\t        if trimmed.is_empty() {\n805\t            return false;\n806\t        }\n807\t\n808\t        let first_char = trimmed.chars().next().unwrap();\n809\t\n810\t        // Exclude lines that start with YAML special characters\n811\t        if matches!(first_char, '-' | ':' | '?' | '|' | '>' | '#' | '[' | ']' | '{' | '}' | '\"' | '\\'' | '!' | '&' | '*' | '%' | '@' | '`') {\n812\t            return false;\n813\t        }\n814\t\n815\t        // Look for word characters at the start\n816\t        trimmed.chars().next().map_or(false, |c| c.is_alphanumeric() || c == '_' || c == '.')\n817\t    }\n818\t}\n819\t\n820\timpl Default for SyntaxDetector {\n821\t    fn default() -> Self {\n822\t        Self::new()\n823\t    }\n824\t}\n825\t\n826\t#[cfg(test)]\n827\tmod tests {\n828\t    use super::*;\n829\t\n830\t    #[test]\n831\t    fn test_detect_mixed_indentation() {\n832\t        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n833\t        let yaml = \"key: value\\n\\t  bad: value\";\n834\t        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n835\t\n836\t        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n837\t        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"mixed tabs and spaces\")));\n838\t    }\n839\t\n840\t    #[test]\n841\t    fn test_detect_missing_colon() {\n842\t        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n843\t        let yaml = \"key value\\n  key2: value2\";\n844\t        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n845\t\n846\t        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n847\t        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"missing colon\")));\n848\t    }\n849\t\n850\t    #[test]\n851\t    fn test_detect_unmatched_bracket() {\n852\t        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n853\t        let yaml = \"key: [value1, value2\";\n854\t        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n855\t\n856\t        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n857\t        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"unclosed\")));\n858\t    }\n859\t\n860\t    #[test]\n861\t    fn test_valid_yaml_no_errors() {\n862\t        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n863\t        let yaml = \"key: value\\n  nested:\\n    - item1\\n    - item2\";\n864\t        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n865\t\n866\t        assert!(errors.is_empty());\n867\t    }\n868\t\n869\t    #[test]\n870\t    fn test_duplicate_key_in_same_scope() {\n871\t        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n872\t        let yaml = \"key: value1\\nkey: value2\";\n873\t        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n874\t\n875\t        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n876\t        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"duplicate key\") && e.message.contains(\"'key'\")));\n877\t    }\n878\t\n879\t    #[test]\n880\t    fn test_same_key_in_different_scopes() {\n881\t        let mut detector =     assert!(dup_errors[0].message.contains(\"'port'\"));\n942\t    }\n943\t\n944\t    #[test]\n945\t    fn test_deeply_nested_scopes() {\n946\t        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n947\t        let yaml = r#\"\n948\tlevel1:\n949\t  level2:\n950\t    level3:\n951\t      key: value1\n952\t  level2b:\n953\t    level3:\n954\t      key: value2\n955\t\"#;\n956\t        let errors =
